  • index

  • Contacts API documentation

Contacts API documentation¶

Add your content using reStructuredText syntax. See the reStructuredText documentation for details.

Main¶

async app.main.add_process_time_header(request: Request, call_next)¶

Add an X-Process-Time header showing how long the request took.

Parameters:

  • request – Incoming HTTP request.

  • call_next – The next handler in the middleware chain.

Returns:

The response, with the processing time header added.

async app.main.global_exception_handler(request: Request, exc: Exception)¶

Catch-all handler for unhandled exceptions.

Parameters:

  • request – Incoming HTTP request.

  • exc – The unhandled exception.

Returns:

A generic 500 error response.

async app.main.healthcheck()¶

Simple healthcheck endpoint.

Returns:

A status message confirming the API is running.

async app.main.http_exception_handler(request: Request, exc: HTTPException)¶

Format HTTPException responses as a consistent error JSON shape.

Parameters:

  • request – Incoming HTTP request.

  • exc – The raised HTTPException.

Returns:

A JSON response with error details.

app.main.lifespan(app: FastAPI)¶

Manage application startup and shutdown.

Closes the database session manager when the app shuts down.

Parameters:

app – The FastAPI application instance.

async app.main.validation_exception_handler(request: Request, exc: RequestValidationError)¶

Format request validation errors as a consistent error JSON shape.

Parameters:

  • request – Incoming HTTP request.

  • exc – The raised validation error.

Returns:

A JSON response with validation error details.

Auth API¶

class app.api.auth.AuthenticationResponse(*, access_token: str, token_type: str)¶

Bases: BaseModel

access_token: str¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

token_type: str¶
async app.api.auth.forgot_password(request: Request, body: ForgotPasswordRequest, db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None))¶

Request a password reset link by email.

Always returns a generic success message, regardless of whether the email exists, to avoid leaking which addresses are registered.

Parameters:

  • request – Incoming HTTP request, used to build the reset link.

  • body – Request body containing the user’s email.

  • db – Database session.

Returns:

A generic confirmation message.

app.api.auth.get_service(db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → UserService¶
async app.api.auth.login_user(form_data: OAuth2PasswordRequestForm = Depends(dependency=None, use_cache=True, scope=None), service: UserService = Depends(dependency=<function get_service>, use_cache=True, scope=None))¶

Authenticate a user and issue a JWT access token.

Parameters:

  • form_data – Login credentials (username is the email, password).

  • service – User service for authentication logic.

Returns:

An access token and its type.

Raises:

HTTPException – If the email is not verified.

async app.api.auth.reset_password(token: str, body: ResetPasswordRequest, db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None))¶

Reset a user’s password using a signed reset token.

Also invalidates the user’s Redis cache entry so the next request picks up the updated password.

Parameters:

  • token – JWT reset token sent to the user’s email.

  • body – Request body containing the new password.

  • db – Database session.

Returns:

A confirmation message.

Raises:

HTTPException – If the token is invalid/expired or the user doesn’t exist.

async app.api.auth.signup_user(request: Request, user_data: UserCreate, service: UserService = Depends(dependency=<function get_service>, use_cache=True, scope=None))¶

Register a new user and send an email verification link.

Parameters:

  • request – Incoming HTTP request, used to build the verification link.

  • user_data – New user’s registration data.

  • service – User service for registration logic.

Returns:

The newly created user.

async app.api.auth.verify_email(token: str, db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None))¶

Verify a user’s email address using a signed token.

Parameters:

  • token – JWT verification token sent to the user’s email.

  • db – Database session.

Returns:

A confirmation message.

Raises:

HTTPException – If the token is invalid/expired or the user doesn’t exist.

Users API¶

async app.api.users.get_me(request: Request, current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None))¶

Get the currently authenticated user’s profile.

Rate-limited to 5 requests per minute per client.

Parameters:

  • request – Incoming HTTP request (required by the rate limiter).

  • current_user – The authenticated user.

Returns:

The current user’s profile data.

async app.api.users.update_avatar_user(file: UploadFile = File(PydanticUndefined), current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function require_admin>, use_cache=True, scope=None), db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None))¶

Upload and set a new avatar image for the current user.

Restricted to admin users only.

Parameters:

  • file – Image file to upload.

  • current_user – The authenticated admin user.

  • db – Database session.

Returns:

The updated user profile, including the new avatar URL.

Raises:

HTTPException – If the current user is not an admin.

Contacts API¶

async app.api.contacts.create_contact(body: ContactModel, service: ContactService = Depends(dependency=<function get_service>, use_cache=True, scope=None), current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None))¶

Create a new contact for the current user.

Parameters:

  • body – Contact data to create.

  • service – Contact service for the creation logic.

  • current_user – The authenticated user who will own the contact.

Returns:

The newly created contact.

Raises:

HTTPException – If a contact with this email already exists.

async app.api.contacts.delete_contact(contact_id: int, service: ContactService = Depends(dependency=<function get_service>, use_cache=True, scope=None), current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None))¶

Delete a contact.

Parameters:

  • contact_id – ID of the contact to delete.

  • service – Contact service for the deletion logic.

  • current_user – The authenticated user who owns the contact.

Raises:

HTTPException – If no contact with this ID exists for the user.

async app.api.contacts.get_birthdays(service: ContactService = Depends(dependency=<function get_service>, use_cache=True, scope=None), current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None))¶

List the current user’s contacts with birthdays in the next 7 days.

Parameters:

  • service – Contact service for the lookup.

  • current_user – The authenticated user.

Returns:

A list of contacts with upcoming birthdays.

async app.api.contacts.get_contact(contact_id: int, service: ContactService = Depends(dependency=<function get_service>, use_cache=True, scope=None), current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None))¶

Retrieve a single contact by ID.

Parameters:

  • contact_id – ID of the contact to retrieve.

  • service – Contact service for the lookup.

  • current_user – The authenticated user who owns the contact.

Returns:

The matching contact.

Raises:

HTTPException – If no contact with this ID exists for the user.

async app.api.contacts.get_contacts(first_name: str | None = None, last_name: str | None = None, email: str | None = None, skip: int = 0, limit: int = 10, service: ContactService = Depends(dependency=<function get_service>, use_cache=True, scope=None), current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None))¶

List the current user’s contacts, with optional filtering and pagination.

Parameters:

  • first_name – Filter by exact first name match.

  • last_name – Filter by exact last name match.

  • email – Filter by exact email match.

  • skip – Number of records to skip.

  • limit – Maximum number of records to return.

  • service – Contact service for the lookup.

  • current_user – The authenticated user.

Returns:

A list of matching contacts.

app.api.contacts.get_service(db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → ContactService¶
async app.api.contacts.update_contact(contact_id: int, body: ContactModel, service: ContactService = Depends(dependency=<function get_service>, use_cache=True, scope=None), current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None))¶

Update an existing contact.

Parameters:

  • contact_id – ID of the contact to update.

  • body – Updated contact data.

  • service – Contact service for the update logic.

  • current_user – The authenticated user who owns the contact.

Returns:

The updated contact.

Raises:

HTTPException – If the contact doesn’t exist.

Auth Service¶

class app.services.auth.Hash¶

Bases: object

Provides password hashing and verification using bcrypt.

get_password_hash(password: str)¶

Hash a plain-text password.

Parameters:

password – The password to hash.

Returns:

The hashed password.

pwd_context = <CryptContext>¶
verify_password(plain_password, hashed_password)¶

Check whether a plain-text password matches its hashed version.

Parameters:

  • plain_password – The password provided by the user.

  • hashed_password – The previously hashed password from the database.

Returns:

True if the password is correct, False otherwise.

app.services.auth.create_access_token(data: dict, expires_delta: float | None = None)¶

Generate a JWT access token.

Parameters:

  • data – Payload to encode in the token (e.g. {“sub”: email}).

  • expires_delta – Token lifetime in seconds. Defaults to 15 minutes.

Returns:

Encoded JWT token as a string.

async app.services.auth.get_current_user(token: str = Depends(dependency=<fastapi.security.oauth2.OAuth2PasswordBearer object>, use_cache=True, scope=None), db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → UserResponse¶

Retrieve the currently authenticated user from the JWT token.

Checks Redis cache first; falls back to the database on a cache miss and stores the result in Redis for subsequent requests.

Parameters:

  • token – JWT access token from the Authorization header.

  • db – Database session.

Returns:

The authenticated user.

Raises:

HTTPException – If the token is invalid or the user doesn’t exist.

app.services.auth.require_admin(current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None))¶

Restrict access to admin users only.

Parameters:

current_user – The currently authenticated user.

Returns:

The user, if they have the admin role.

Raises:

HTTPException – If the user’s role is not “admin” (403 Forbidden).

User Service¶

class app.services.users.UserService(repository: UserRepository)¶

Bases: object

Handles user registration, authentication, and avatar updates.

async auth_user(email: str, password: str)¶

Authenticate a user by email and password.

Parameters:

  • email – User’s email address.

  • password – Plain-text password to verify.

Returns:

The authenticated user.

Raises:

HTTPException – If credentials are invalid, or on a database error.

async register_user(data: UserCreate)¶

Register a new user.

Parameters:

data – New user’s registration data (email, password).

Returns:

The newly created user.

Raises:

HTTPException – If a user with this email already exists, or on a database error.

async update_avatar_url(email: str, url: str)¶

Update a user’s avatar URL.

Parameters:

  • email – Email of the user to update.

  • url – New avatar URL (e.g. from Cloudinary).

Returns:

The updated user.

Contact Service¶

class app.services.contacts.ContactService(repository: ContactRepository)¶

Bases: object

Handles contact CRUD operations and birthday lookups for a user.

async create_contact(body: ContactModel, current_user: User)¶

Create a new contact for the current user.

Parameters:

  • body – Contact data to create.

  • current_user – The authenticated user who will own the contact.

Returns:

The newly created contact.

Raises:

HTTPException – If a contact with this email already exists, or on a database error.

async delete_contact(id: int, current_user: User)¶

Delete a contact.

Parameters:

  • id – ID of the contact to delete.

  • current_user – The authenticated user who owns the contact.

Returns:

The deleted contact.

Raises:

HTTPException – If no contact with this ID exists for the user.

async get_b_days(current_user: User)¶

Retrieve contacts whose birthday falls within the upcoming period.

Parameters:

current_user – The authenticated user whose contacts are checked.

Returns:

A list of contacts with upcoming birthdays.

async get_contact(id: int, current_user: User)¶

Retrieve a single contact by ID.

Parameters:

  • id – ID of the contact to retrieve.

  • current_user – The authenticated user who owns the contact.

Returns:

The matching contact.

Raises:

HTTPException – If no contact with this ID exists for the user.

async get_contacts(first_name: str | None, last_name: str | None, email: str | None, skip: int, limit: int, current_user: User)¶

Retrieve a paginated, optionally filtered list of the user’s contacts.

Parameters:

  • first_name – Filter by first name (partial match).

  • last_name – Filter by last name (partial match).

  • email – Filter by email (partial match).

  • skip – Number of records to skip (for pagination).

  • limit – Maximum number of records to return.

  • current_user – The authenticated user whose contacts are returned.

Returns:

A list of matching contacts.

async update_contact(id: int, body: ContactModel, current_user: User)¶

Update an existing contact.

Parameters:

  • id – ID of the contact to update.

  • body – Updated contact data.

  • current_user – The authenticated user who owns the contact.

Returns:

The updated contact.

Raises:

HTTPException – If the contact doesn’t exist, or on a database error.

Email Service¶

async app.services.email.send_password_reset_email(email: EmailStr, host: str, token: str)¶

Send a password reset link to a user.

Parameters:

  • email – Recipient’s email address.

  • host – Base URL of the API, used to build the reset link.

  • token – JWT password reset token.

async app.services.email.send_verification_email(email: EmailStr, host: str, token: str)¶

Send an email verification link to a newly registered user.

Parameters:

  • email – Recipient’s email address.

  • host – Base URL of the API, used to build the verification link.

  • token – JWT verification token.

Upload File Service¶

class app.services.upload_file.UploadFileService(cloud_name, api_key, api_secret)¶

Bases: object

Handles uploading and resizing user avatar images via Cloudinary.

static upload_file(file, username) → str¶

Upload a file to Cloudinary and return a resized image URL.

Parameters:

  • file – The uploaded file object.

  • username – Used to build a unique public ID for the image.

Returns:

URL of the uploaded image, cropped to 250x250.

User Repository¶

class app.repository.users.UserRepository(db: AsyncSession)¶

Bases: object

Provides database access for user records.

async create_user(data: UserCreate, hashed_pass: str) → User¶

Create a new user record.

Parameters:

  • data – Registration data containing the user’s email.

  • hashed_pass – Already-hashed password to store.

Returns:

The newly created user.

async get_user_by_email(email: str) → User | None¶

Find a user by their email address.

Parameters:

email – Email address to search for.

Returns:

The matching user, or None if not found.

async update_avatar_url(email: str, url: str) → User¶

Update a user’s avatar URL.

Parameters:

  • email – Email of the user to update.

  • url – New avatar URL.

Returns:

The updated user.

async verify_user_email(email: str) → None¶

Mark a user’s email as verified.

Parameters:

email – Email address of the user to verify.

Contact Repository¶

class app.repository.contacts.ContactRepository(session: AsyncSession)¶

Bases: object

Provides database access for contact records, scoped to a specific user.

async create_contact(body: ContactModel, user_id: int)¶

Create a new contact for a user.

Parameters:

  • body – Contact data to create.

  • user_id – ID of the user who will own the contact.

Returns:

The newly created contact.

async delete_contact(id: int, user_id: int)¶

Delete a contact.

Parameters:

  • id – ID of the contact to delete.

  • user_id – ID of the contact’s owner.

Returns:

The deleted contact, or None if not found.

async get_birthdays(user_id: int)¶

Retrieve a user’s contacts with birthdays in the next 7 days.

Handles the month-boundary case (e.g. late December into January) by splitting the date range across two month conditions.

Parameters:

user_id – ID of the contacts’ owner.

Returns:

A list of contacts with upcoming birthdays.

async get_contact_by_id(id: int, user_id: int)¶

Find a single contact by ID, scoped to its owner.

Parameters:

  • id – ID of the contact to retrieve.

  • user_id – ID of the contact’s owner.

Returns:

The matching contact, or None if not found.

async get_contacts(user_id: int, first_name: str | None = None, last_name: str | None = None, email: str | None = None, skip: int = 0, limit: int = 10)¶

Retrieve a paginated, optionally filtered list of a user’s contacts.

Parameters:

  • user_id – ID of the contacts’ owner.

  • first_name – Filter by exact first name match.

  • last_name – Filter by exact last name match.

  • email – Filter by exact email match.

  • skip – Number of records to skip (for pagination).

  • limit – Maximum number of records to return.

Returns:

A list of matching contacts.

async update_contact(id: int, body: ContactModel, user_id: int)¶

Update an existing contact’s fields.

Parameters:

  • id – ID of the contact to update.

  • body – Updated contact data.

  • user_id – ID of the contact’s owner.

Returns:

The updated contact, or None if not found.

Database Models¶

class app.db.models.Base(**kwargs: Any)¶

Bases: DeclarativeBase

metadata: ClassVar[MetaData] = MetaData()¶

Refers to the _schema.MetaData collection that will be used for new _schema.Table objects.

See also

orm_declarative_metadata

registry: ClassVar[registry] = <sqlalchemy.orm.decl_api.registry object>¶

Refers to the _orm.registry in use where new _orm.Mapper objects will be associated.

class app.db.models.Contact(**kwargs)¶

Bases: Base

Database model for a user’s contact entry.

additional_data: Mapped[str | None]¶
birthday: Mapped[date]¶
email: Mapped[str]¶
first_name: Mapped[str]¶
id: Mapped[int]¶
last_name: Mapped[str]¶
phone: Mapped[str]¶
user: Mapped[User]¶
user_id: Mapped[int]¶
class app.db.models.User(**kwargs)¶

Bases: Base

Database model for an application user.

avatar_url: Mapped[str | None]¶
contacts: Mapped[list[Contact]]¶
email: Mapped[str]¶
id: Mapped[int]¶
is_verified: Mapped[bool]¶
password: Mapped[str]¶
role: Mapped[UserRole]¶
class app.db.models.UserRole(*values)¶

Bases: str, Enum

Available user roles: regular user or admin.

admin = 'admin'¶
user = 'user'¶
